origin.capability_match: Control Interchain Accounts on remote chains (doc_20)

attack_surface.data_description: Interchain messages for ICA control, traversing from Deployer (Actor 12) through Mailboxes (Component 102, 103) via Relayers (Actor 3).

security_gap.gap_description: Relayers might censor messages, or Mailbox contracts could be congested, preventing Deployer's commands from reaching the ICA.

actor_risk.description: The Deployer (Actor 12) relies on the availability of the interchain messaging system to control its ICAs.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Inability to control an ICA can disrupt its intended operations or lock funds/functionality.

assumptions: Relayers (Actor 3) can selectively choose not to relay messages for Deployer (Actor 12).
Mailbox contracts (Component 102, 103) can become unavailable due to network congestion or attacks.
The Interchain Account (Component 112) itself does not have internal DoS vulnerabilities preventing command processing.

mitigation.strategy: Promote a decentralized and permissionless relayer network. Design robust Mailbox contracts. Implement ISMs that allow for alternative message delivery paths or challenge periods for censorship.

description: An attacker or failing infrastructure prevents the Deployer (Actor 12) from sending or having commands executed by its Interchain Account (Component 112), by censoring messages or DDoSing critical messaging components.

trace_source.matched_capability: Control Interchain Accounts on remote chains (doc_20)
